news: AFCON 2025 winners momentum; the Argentina opener is the marquee tie of Group J.
2026-06-27: Amoura OUT with hamstring injury; Algeria must beat Austria (who have superior GD) to guarantee R32 spot — a draw may not be enough.

Austria.form_note: Won 3-1 vs Jordan. Lost 0-2 to Argentina (both Messi goals set new WC scoring record). Austria 3pts in Group J — final game vs Algeria June 27 (Algeria also on 3pts; GD favors Austria, so a draw advances Austria).

injuries.note: Right thigh injury in pre-tournament warm-up vs Tunisia; required surgery — ruled out of entire tournament, replaced in squad by Ljubicic

Broken jaw suffered vs Jordan opener (collision); initially feared tournament over, but fitted with protective brace — Rangnick confirmed June 21 'He is ready to play tomorrow just like any other player'; available for Argentina match

key_players.note: Captain at his first World Cup
All-time record scorer; broke the record during qualification
20-year-old winger; 3 goals in 22 appearances in a productive first PSV season

news: Ralf Rangnick's pressing side is a popular pick to join Argentina out of Group J.
2026-06-22 pre-match: Posch cleared to play despite broken jaw (protective brace fitted; went through full training). Baumgartner (surgery) out for tournament; Ljubicic called up as replacement.
2026-06-27: Straight shootout with Algeria for 2nd place; a draw is enough for Austria (superior GD). Algeria's Amoura OUT with hamstring injury — weakens their attack.

Jordan.form_note: Lost 1-3 to Austria. Lost 1-2 to Algeria (led 1-0 through Al-Rashdan, but Algeria equalized and won 82'). Jordan eliminated from Group J. Last game vs Argentina June 27.

injuries.note: Injured since December; top scorer in qualifying with 8 goals — ruled out of the entire tournament
Injury; ruled out of the tournament

key_players.note: Star winger from the 2023 Asian Cup final run

news: Qualified automatically by finishing second behind South Korea in their AFC group.

Portugal.form_note: Drew 1-1 with DR Congo in Group K opener (Wissa stoppage-time goal for DRC). Won 5-0 vs Uzbekistan (June 23, Ronaldo brace — becomes first player to score at six World Cup editions; Nuno Mendes free-kick, Nematov OG, Leão). Portugal second in Group K. Next vs Colombia June 27.

injuries.note: Recovered from minor injury that ruled him out of the DR Congo opener; available and expected to start vs Uzbekistan (June 23)
Not available for the Uzbekistan match (June 23)

key_players.note: At 41 becomes the first man to play six World Cups; already the only player to score at five World Cups; chasing the one trophy that has eluded him
Arrives straight from winning the Champions League with PSG
Champions League winner; core of Portugal's elite midfield
Part of a deep forward group with Trincao and Goncalo Ramos behind Ronaldo
Widely rated the best left-back in the world after PSG's CL triumph

news: Roberto Martinez named the squad May 19; a symbolic squad place was dedicated to the late Diogo Jota.
Reserve keeper Ricardo Velho travels as extra cover.
Group K with Colombia, Uzbekistan and DR Congo — strong favorites to top it.
2026-06-23: Ronaldo started and scored twice vs Uzbekistan (5-0 win), becoming the first player ever to score at six World Cup editions. Nuno Mendes, Leão also scored.

DR Congo.form_note: Drew 1-1 with Portugal (Wissa stoppage-time equalizer). Lost 0-1 to Colombia (June 23, Muñoz 76'). DR Congo 1pt. Last game vs Uzbekistan June 27 (eliminated).

injuries.note: Injury; unavailable for Colombia match (June 23)

news: Won the African playoff path then the FIFA intercontinental playoff to reach a first finals in 52 years.

2026-06-23 pre-match: Théo Bongonda OUT injured; Wissa and Bakambu lead the attack. Predicted XI (5-3-2): Mpasi-Nzau; Mbemba, Tuanzebe, Kapuadi, Wan-Bissaka, Masuaku; Sadiki, Moutoussamy, Kayembe; Bakambu, Wissa.

Uzbekistan.form_note: Lost 1-3 to Colombia in WC opener (June 17). Lost 0-5 to Portugal (June 23) — Ronaldo brace, Nuno Mendes, Leão, OG. Uzbekistan eliminated from Group K.

injuries.note: Back injury; missed Colombia opener and tournament
Missed last warm-up friendly; fitness uncertain
Came off during the Colombia defeat; slight doubt for Portugal match (June 23)

key_players.note: Centre-back and the squad's highest-profile player
Record scorer leading the line

news: Group K finale vs Colombia likely decides their knockout hopes.

Colombia.form_note: Won 3-1 vs Uzbekistan (June 17). Won 1-0 vs DR Congo (June 23, Guadalajara — Muñoz 76'; Diaz denied a brace late on). Colombia 6pts, through to R32, top of Group K. Next vs Portugal June 27.

injuries.note: Missed the Uzbekistan opener with thigh discomfort; fully recovered (100%) and available vs DR Congo as attacking option

key_players.note: Undisputed leader and Colombia's top scorer in qualifying; key creative force at Bayern
34-year-old talisman left his club May 13 to join camp early; denied retirement rumors
Among the striker options in a deep attacking pool

news: Group K schedule: Uzbekistan in Mexico City, DR Congo in Guadalajara, Portugal in Miami.
Diaz and James were integral throughout the qualifying campaign and headline the 26.
2026-06-23: Won 1-0 vs DR Congo (Muñoz 76'); Diaz denied a brace late. Colombia top Group K with 6pts and through to R32. Next vs Portugal June 27 in Miami.

England.form_note: Won 4-2 vs Croatia (Kane 2, Bellingham, Rashford). Drew 0-0 vs Ghana (June 23, Foxborough) — Kane went closest late on but Three Lions held. England 4pts, top of Group L. Next vs Panama June 27.

injuries.note: Hamstring injury from late-April Merseyside derby
Season-ending foot surgery; missed last four months
Calf injury sustained in training; ruled out of the tournament, replaced by Trevoh Chalobah (Chelsea)
Managing neural pain in hamstring; was fit for Ghana match but England assessing ahead of Panama (June 27)
Being assessed ahead of Panama match (June 27) — fitness in doubt
Achilles being managed throughout; started vs Ghana and expected to continue vs Panama
Hamstring discomfort managed as precaution vs Ghana; expected to start vs Panama (June 27) per predicted XI

key_players.note: 36 goals in 31 games as Bayern won the title; European Golden Shoe winner averaging a goal every 66.1 minutes in the Bundesliga; captains England at a record-equalling third World Cup
Handed the No 10 shirt; club form indifferent with injury concerns this season but undroppable
Established No 1 behind a defense that didn't concede in qualifying
Handed a key shirt number in Tuchel's squad reveal

news: Thomas Tuchel's ruthless squad cuts: Cole Palmer, Phil Foden, Trent Alexander-Arnold, Harry Maguire and Adam Wharton all left out — biggest selection story of the build-up.
Ivan Toney and Ollie Watkins named as the back-up strikers to Kane.
ESPN power-rankings panel gave England zero first-place votes amid uncertainty over Tuchel's selections despite the flawless qualifying record.
2026-06-27 pre-match: Declan Rice and Reece James both being assessed ahead of Panama; Carrasquilla (Panama, adductor) OUT — win seals Group L top spot for England.

Croatia.form_note: Lost 2-4 to England. Won 1-0 vs Panama (June 23, Toronto — Ante Budimir winner; Luka Modric played his 200th international cap). Croatia 3pts, alive in Group L. Next vs Ghana June 27.

injuries.note: Recovered from broken shinbone surgery
Recovered from facial/cheekbone fracture in time for the tournament
Missed both pre-tournament friendlies with minor injury; returned to full training and expected in the squad vs England

key_players.note: At 40 still playing 90 minutes weekly for Milan; record sixth World Cup, 2018 Golden Ball winner
Defensive cornerstone, back fit after shinbone surgery
One of four centurions in the squad alongside Modric, Perisic and Kramaric
Veteran attacking outlet, also past 100 caps

news: ESPN backs the Vatreni to reach the knockout stage from Group L with England, Ghana and Panama.
Croatia's 2026 World Cup jersey was ranked last by The Athletic, prompting a defense from the federation.

Ghana.form_note: Won 1-0 vs Panama (June 17). Drew 0-0 vs England (June 23, Foxborough) — dogged display, England frustrated throughout. Ghana 4pts, top of Group L alongside England. Next vs Panama June 27 (must win to advance).

injuries.note: Long-term injury rules out Ghana's biggest star
Long-term injury
Previously listed out due to visa complications; cleared to enter the USA — available and expected to start vs England (June 23), demotes Elisha Owusu to bench
Groin injury vs Panama; returned to individual training June 23 and in contention, but still a doubt for England clash — Benjamin Asare on standby to start

key_players.note: Carries the main attacking burden with Kudus out

news: Losing Kudus and Salisu severely dents hopes of escaping Group L.
Thomas Partey cleared to play vs England — his visa complication resolved; significant midfield upgrade.
GK Lawrence Ati Zigi (groin) returned to individual training June 23 and is in contention, but still a doubt — Benjamin Asare on standby.

Panama.form_note: Lost 0-1 to Ghana. Lost 0-1 to Croatia (June 23, Toronto — Budimir goal, Modric 200th cap). Panama eliminated from Group L. Last game vs England June 27.

injuries.note: Groin injury; MISSED the Ghana match; still doubtful for Croatia game — out for almost a month before partial return to training

key_players.note: Midfield orchestrator of the CONCACAF qualifying campaign

news: Topped their CONCACAF final-round group to qualify directly.
2026-06-23 pre-match: Carlos Harvey and Cesar Blackman both on yellow card warnings — one more booking for either means missing the final group game vs Ghana.
